contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Governor admin pre-blocks + print-cutoff fix + DAPP_PAGE_CONVENTIONS §9b/§16
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dapp_beta/pull/13
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/245
  
  Details:
  Governor admin pre-blocks + print-cutoff convention, 2026-05-29.
  
  Three asks in one batch: (a) governor_contributor_admin.html should show the signed payload + Edgar response in <pre> blocks, similar to report_contribution.html; (b) #descriptionInput cuts off in print mode, fix dapp-wide; (c) document the conventions for future LLM sessions.
  
  PRs delivered:
  
  1. dapp_beta#13 — governor_contributor_admin.html now follows the new §9b convention: short success status line + <div id='submissionResult'> with two <pre> blocks (Transaction request — the verbatim signed share text; Edgar response — JSON.stringify(result, null, 2)). signAndSubmit() refactored to return { result, shareText } so the caller renders both without re-deriving the payload. .textContent (not .innerHTML) used on the <pre>s for safe handling of < > & literals in contributor names. Same PR adds the §16 print stylesheet to both governor_contributor_admin.html AND report_contribution.html: @media print rules that force textareas to height:auto + overflow:visible + page-break-inside:auto, plus parallel pre rules. Fixes Gary's reported #descriptionInput print cutoff and prevents the same bug on the new submissionResult <pre>s.
  
  2. agentic_ai_context#245 — DAPP_PAGE_CONVENTIONS.md adds §9b (submission result rendering with reference implementation + .textContent safety note + status-line-is-additive guidance) and §16 (print stylesheet block + per-page inline rationale since no shared main.css exists yet). Both sections include backlog notes pointing future sessions at non-conforming pages to fix opportunistically.
  
  Net: every new dapp page that lands after this knows to show signed payload + response in <pre>s and inherit the print rules. Existing pages with the bug are a backlog item rather than a code freeze.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
